title: SQG Identity: A Lean 4 Formalization of Fourier-Space Identities for the Surface Quasi-Geostrophic Equation with Conditional Regularity Roadmap
description: A Lean 4 + mathlib formalization of Fourier-space identities for the inviscid Surface Quasi-Geostrophic equation on the 2-torus, together with a conditional regularity roadmap. Two theorems are machine-verified unconditionally: the shear-vorticity identity (the combination S_nt − ω/2 has Fourier multiplier |k|·sin²(α−β) and vanishes identically for one-dimensional fronts) and the selection-rule bound (pointwise mode-level inequality with equality characterization, lifted to an integrated Sobolev-seminorm form via Parseval). Supporting infrastructure includes Riesz transforms on the torus via Fourier multipliers, Leray–Helmholtz projection, the fractional Sobolev scale, a self-adjoint Biot–Savart factorization, tight mode-level identities for the strain and vorticity tensors, and an α-fractional heat semigroup with smoothing and contractivity bounds plus a parallel suite for the classical heat semigroup. A conditional form of the SQG regularity theorem is formalized as named Lean structures for the fractional Sobolev calculus, material maximum principle, BKM criterion, and SQG evolution axioms; uniform Sobolev bounds follow from these hypotheses. v0.6.0 adds §14 labeled hypothesis structures HasStrainLowerBound, HasBoundaryCurvatureBound, and HasThermostatBound matching the paper's (H-strain), (H-bdry), and (H-α) hypotheses (paper §9.6.3 and §9.8), with MaterialMaxPrinciple.of_HstrainHbdry and .of_thermostat constructors. §13 adds the lattice Sobolev per-mode Ḣˢ sup bound. §B.15 adds the inverse Fourier transform lpOfFourierCoeff via mathlib's mFourierBasis. §B.16 through §B.19 add a machine-verified Rellich-Kondrachov compact embedding H¹(𝕋²) ⊂⊂ L² in Fourier form, including a from-scratch proof of the countable-family Cantor diagonal extraction and the capstone fourier_rellich_kondrachov. SqgIdentity/FourierBridge.lean is a new 2,490-line module wiring the companion sqg-lean-proofs-fourier package (classical Littlewood-Paley, Bony paraproducts, quantitative uniform-in-N Kato-Ponce commutator, homogeneous Sobolev infrastructure, approximately 2,800 lines) and delivering HasSqgGalerkinAllSBound.ofGalerkin_nonZero_fullyConcrete, an end-to-end Path B chain composed of concrete discharges of L² conservation, Riesz velocity preservation, Ḣˢ energy identity, velocity Lipschitz-sup via Sobolev embedding, and exponential Grönwall closure, verified conditional on the paper's (H-strain) + (H-bdry) or equivalently (H-α). The accompanying paper is revised in this release to explicitly state Theorem 3 as conditional on these hypotheses, with a new §7.4 comparing the hypothesis pair against prior SQG conditional regularity results (Constantin-Majda-Tabak 1994, Córdoba 1998, He-Kiselev 2021, Kiselev-Ryzhik-Yao-Zlatoš 2016, Jeong-Kim 2024). The release comprises 29,049 lines of Lean 4 source project-wide with zero `sorry` occurrences and no axioms beyond mathlib; the build is reproducible with a single `lake build` against mathlib v4.29.0. The accompanying paper deriving the identity, selection rule, and conditional regularity theorem is distributed with the release.
